name: Lake Michigan (Indiana Waters)
signatureSpecies: King salmon, coho salmon, steelhead, lake trout

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.size: 10-25 lb avg, 30+ lb in fall stage
species.notes: Stocked annually by IDNR. Concentrate around tributary mouths Aug-Oct as fish stage to spawn.

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: Spring near-shore April-June 50-58°F water. Major IDNR stocking program.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Trib runs Sep-Apr. Skamania strain (summer) + winter strain.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Deep-water summer (60-100 ft); shoreline rocky structure in spring + fall.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Near-shore in spring + harbor mouths fall through winter.

species.name: Yellow perch
species.notes: Indiana's classic shoreline fish — historically the bread-and-butter of pier anglers. Populations cyclical.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Rocky breakwaters + harbor piers; June-September.

patterns.title: Spring coho near-shore
patterns.technique: Trolling small Cleos, Husky Jerks, Trout Magnets, dodger + peanut fly combos
patterns.where: Within 1/4 mi of shore, 8-25 ft depth
patterns.details: Stained run-off water along the south shore concentrates fish. Crank baits in chartreuse / orange / silver dominate.

patterns.title: Summer king salmon offshore
patterns.technique: Downrigger trolling — flasher + Action Fly, J-plug, Spin Doctor + meat rigs
patterns.where: 60-120 ft of water, 50-90 ft down at the thermocline
patterns.details: Set a temperature probe; target the 48-52°F band where kings hold.

patterns.title: Fall salmon staging at trib mouths
patterns.technique: Casting spoons + spinners from piers; bobbers with skein; trolling tight to harbor mouths
patterns.where: Trail Creek, Burns Ditch, East Chicago, Hammond, Michigan City piers
patterns.details: Skein, spawn sacs, salmon eggs, large spinners, jointed Rapalas. Dawn and dusk are peak.

patterns.title: Pier perch in summer
patterns.technique: Bobber + minnow / fathead; light tip-down rigs with crawler chunks
patterns.where: Hammond Marina, East Chicago Marina, Whihala Beach pier, Michigan City pier
patterns.details: Perch populations swing year-to-year; check IDNR creel reports before driving.

patterns.title: Lake trout structure season
patterns.technique: Vertical jigging with Swedish Pimples + large tube jigs; trolling 3-color leadcore with dodger + spoons
patterns.where: Rocky bottom 30-80 ft; offshore reefs
patterns.details: IDNR ramped stocking dramatically post-2010; Indiana lake-trout fishery is far stronger than most anglers realize.

notes: Indiana's 45-mile Lake Michigan shoreline is one of the most accessible salmonid fisheries in the Midwest. The shore-fishing scene from Hammond to Michigan City is genuinely world-class in the right window.

name: Trail Creek

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run mid-Sep through October. Peak around the first major cool-water push.

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: Fall run overlaps king run, smaller numbers.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Skamania summer-run starts in July; winter strain Nov-Apr.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Fall + winter stragglers up the creek.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Summer residential bass in deeper pools.

patterns.title: Fall king run swung flies + bobber-dogged eggs
patterns.technique: Float rigs with skein / spawn sacs; large streamers swung on indicator setups; spinners
patterns.where: From the mouth to upstream pools; structure-holding fish
patterns.details: Heavy 12-15 lb mainlines, 10 lb fluoro leader. Crowds peak weekends — early weekday is best.

patterns.title: Skamania summer steelhead
patterns.technique: Small spinners (size 2-3 Mepps Aglia, blue fox), small streamers in cold pools
patterns.where: Deep cold pools below shaded sections
patterns.details: Skamania strain is a Lake Michigan rarity that originated in Indiana — these are agressive summer chrome.

patterns.title: Winter steelhead
patterns.technique: Float-rig with sucker spawn / yarn flies / fresh skein; nymphs under indicator
patterns.where: Slow tail-outs + deep pools; pocket water above bridges
patterns.details: Use heavier flies for cold-water fish that won't move far. Pink + chartreuse stand out in stained winter water.

regulations: Indiana salmon/trout stamp required. Specific seasonal bag and size regulations — consult current IDNR rules.
notes: Indiana's most-fished salmon and steelhead trib. The fish ladder at Hwy 35 controls upstream passage. Crowded but productive. The Skamania-strain summer steelhead is genuinely an Indiana specialty.

name: Little Calumet River (East Branch)

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run Sep-Oct via Burns Ditch / Portage Burns Waterway.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Both Skamania summer + winter strain.

patterns.title: Burns Ditch fall salmon
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs under float, large spoons, Cleos, jointed Rapala
patterns.where: Burns Waterway mouth, channel to first riffle
patterns.details: Salmon stage at the channel where Indiana Dunes State Park borders. Heavy spawning activity below the dam.

patterns.title: Winter steelhead Little Cal
patterns.technique: Bobber-dogging spawn / yarn flies; nymphing through pools
patterns.where: Deep pools through Chesterton + Portage stretches
patterns.details: Lower flows than Trail Creek so reads like a smaller stream. Less pressured upstream of US 20.

regulations: Salmon / trout stamp required. Standard Lake Michigan trib regs.
notes: Second-most-fished Indiana salmon trib after Trail Creek. The Burns Waterway mouth (Portage Burns) is one of the most reliable fall salmon spots in the state.

name: St. Joseph River (South Bend)
signatureSpecies: King salmon, coho salmon, steelhead, smallmouth bass

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run staging below the East Race + Twin Branch dam.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Year-round in tailwaters; Skamania summer + winter strain.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Year-round resident; warm-water months are prime.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Below dams + the East Race tailwater Nov-Apr.

patterns.title: Fall salmon at Twin Branch + East Race
patterns.technique: Floats with skein, spinners, large spoons; flies — egg patterns + estaz worms
patterns.where: Twin Branch Dam tailwater, East Race Waterway, Central Park stretch
patterns.details: Mishawaka's tailwater is the most reliable concentrated salmon hole in the state — 30-pound kings stage in the bowl below the dam.

patterns.title: Year-round steelhead tailwater
patterns.technique: Drift-fishing nymphs + eggs; swinging soft-hackles + small streamers
patterns.where: Twin Branch tailwater, East Race, Central Park, Riverside Drive
patterns.details: Cold dam discharges keep summer fish in even when Trail Creek is hot. Six Span (Mishawaka) is a public-access classic.

patterns.title: Smallmouth ladder-and-rocks
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, soft jerkbaits, Rapala Shad Raps
patterns.where: Rocky riprap below dams, current breaks, gravel bars
patterns.details: St. Joe smallmouth average 12-15 in with 3-4 lb fish common. The deep current seams below Twin Branch hold the biggest fish.

regulations: Salmon / trout stamp required for salmonids. Smallmouth statewide regs.
notes: Technically the St. Joseph River flows into Lake Michigan at St. Joseph, MI, but the South Bend / Mishawaka section is Indiana's most reliable river salmon fishery — fish surge upstream to the Twin Branch Dam and stack.

name: Burns Ditch / Portage Burns Waterway

patterns.title: Fall pier and harbor salmon
patterns.technique: Cleos, spinners, jointed Rapalas; spawn sacs under floats
patterns.where: Burns Waterway pier, mouth, channel up to the Hwy 12 bridge
patterns.details: One of the most productive shore-access salmon spots in Indiana when the run is on.

patterns.title: Winter steelhead through the channel
patterns.technique: Float rigs with spawn / yarn / sucker spawn flies
patterns.where: Pools through Portage Riverwalk + Hwy 12 stretch
patterns.details: Lower flow means fish move slowly — slow your presentation accordingly.

notes: Functionally the lower Little Calumet East Branch. The 'Portage Burns Waterway' mouth is THE shore-access salmon spot for NW Indiana.

name: Salt Creek (Porter County)

patterns.title: Lightly-pressured winter steelhead
patterns.technique: Float rigs, yarn flies, small streamers
patterns.where: Pools below Hwy 6 + upstream
patterns.details: Salt Creek is the under-the-radar option when Trail Creek + Little Cal are crowded. Smaller fish runs but materially less pressure.

notes: A tributary of the Little Calumet East Branch — fish run up from Burns Ditch. Best in solitude on weekday winter days.

name: Wolf Lake (Hammond)

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Healthy population; weedlines, docks, riprap.

species.name: Channel catfish
species.notes: Stocked by IDNR; consistent action year-round.

patterns.title: Bank-friendly summer largemouth
patterns.technique: Texas-rigged worms, Senkos, spinnerbaits along weed edges
patterns.where: Riprap walls, dock corners, pad edges
patterns.details: Urban park lake with strong shore access from Wolf Lake Park.

patterns.title: Channel cat after dark
patterns.technique: Cut bait + circle hooks; chicken liver on slip-rigs
patterns.where: Channel between basins; deeper basin edges

notes: Quintessential urban-fishing lake. Three connected basins. Long shore access makes it Lake County's most-fished lake.

name: Cedar Lake (Lake County)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR stocked; surprisingly strong population.

patterns.title: Spring walleye after ice-out
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/8 oz; suspending jerkbaits at dusk
patterns.where: North end weedline edges + the basin point
patterns.details: Cedar Lake is the NW Indiana walleye sleeper — stocking has built a real fishery.

patterns.title: Summer largemouth pad edges
patterns.technique: Frogs over pads; senkos in pockets; chatterbaits on outside edges
patterns.where: South end pad fields; west shore docks

patterns.title: Crappie spring spawn
patterns.technique: 1/16 oz jig + minnow under bobber
patterns.where: Shallow brush + dock pilings around the entire shoreline

access: Cedar Lake Town Boat Launch (north end)
notes: Lake County's premier multi-species lake. Walleye + largemouth combo is rare for NW Indiana.

name: Kankakee River
county: Starke / LaPorte / Jasper / Newton / Lake / Porter
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, channel catfish, flathead catfish

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Statewide-recognized smallmouth river. Rock riprap + gravel bars dominate the action.

species.name: Flathead catfish
species.notes: True trophy class — Kankakee gives up flatheads bigger than most IN waters.

species.name: Northern pike
species.notes: Lower river + sloughs.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Light population — work below dams.

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Sloughs + slow-water sections.

patterns.title: Summer smallmouth at gravel + rock
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, in-line spinners, soft jerkbaits
patterns.where: Below rapids + ledges; gravel bars; current seams below riprap
patterns.details: The Kankakee fishes like a southern smallie river — wading is the high-quality way to fish it.

patterns.title: Big flathead under-cut bank patrol
patterns.technique: Live bluegill / chub on heavy circle hooks; bait-clicker rigs
patterns.where: Deep timber + under-cut banks; outside river bends with structure
patterns.details: Kankakee flatheads run truly large. Use 50 lb braid; tie on a snell.

patterns.title: Fall walleye below dams
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow / fathead at 1/8-1/4 oz
patterns.where: Below the State Line Dam + the small dams in the upper stretch

notes: Indiana's most distinctive river. The 'Grand Marsh of the Kankakee' (now drained) was once one of the great wetlands of North America. What remains is a deceptively fishy smallmouth + catfish system.

name: Iroquois River

patterns.title: Quiet-water smallie wading
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, small crankbaits
patterns.where: Riffle-to-pool transitions; downed timber + gravel
patterns.details: Smaller and more lightly-pressured than the Kankakee.

notes: Major Kankakee tributary. Quiet, lightly pressured, classic Midwest prairie smallmouth water.

name: Lake James (Pokagon State Park)
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, walleye, northern pike, muskie

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Deep clear water — Indiana smallmouth lake at its best.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR stocked; mature self-sustaining fishery.

species.name: Northern pike
species.notes: Weedlines + cabbage flats hold quality fish.

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: IDNR stocked.

patterns.title: Smallmouth on summer deep structure
patterns.technique: Drop-shot with 4" finesse worm; Ned rigs; deep tubes
patterns.where: Mid-lake humps 18-25 ft; rocky points on the third basin (deepest)
patterns.details: Clear water rewards finesse + light fluoro. Match the alewife / spottail shiner forage.

patterns.title: Walleye trolling cabbage edges
patterns.technique: Bottom bouncers + crawler harnesses; jig + minnow on rocky points
patterns.where: Cabbage edges 12-18 ft; transitions on the second basin
patterns.details: Evening + night fishing dominates; troll the points after dark with deep-diving jerkbaits.

patterns.title: Pike + muskie on weed flats
patterns.technique: Bucktails, glide baits, large rubber, jerkbait twitches
patterns.where: Cabbage / coontail edges; classic NE Indiana pike weed flats
patterns.details: Muskie stocking has produced legitimate fish — figure-8s at boatside pay.

regulations: Walleye 14" min, 5/day. Muskie 36" min where applicable. Statewide bass regs.
notes: The crown jewel of Indiana natural lakes. Three connected basins, glacial origin, max depth 80 ft. Pokagon State Park = clean shore-and-launch access.

name: Crooked Lake (Steuben County)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR stocked.

species.name: Cisco (lake herring)
species.notes: One of the few Indiana lakes that still has a native cisco population.

patterns.title: Deep smallmouth on summer humps
patterns.technique: Drop-shot + Ned; tubes
patterns.where: Three-basin transitions; deep rocky humps 18-30 ft

patterns.title: Cisco run February dipping
patterns.technique: Small jigging spoons + waxworms; cisco rigs
patterns.where: Deep basin 50-70 ft
patterns.details: Ice-only fishery in practice. One of very few Hoosier waters where you can target cisco.

notes: Three connected basins with a deep east basin. The cisco population is biologically significant — most other natural lakes lost theirs.

name: Snow Lake

patterns.title: Channel-connection smallmouth
patterns.technique: Jerkbaits, tubes, Ned rigs
patterns.where: Snow-James channel area; deep clear points
patterns.details: Connected to Lake James via channel — fish slide between systems.

access: Pokagon State Park area via channel
notes: Connected to Lake James via a navigable channel. Smaller and quieter than James itself.

name: Hamilton Lake

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Strong stocking + good natural reproduction.

patterns.title: Walleye points + drop-offs
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses, slow-troll jerkbaits, jig + minnow
patterns.where: Main lake points + the deep south basin
patterns.details: Evening + dusk + night dominate for shallow-water walleye.

notes: Underrated walleye lake. Less crowded than James / Crooked.

name: Pleasant Lake

species.name: Bluegill
species.notes: Pleasant Lake bluegill are notably bigger-than-average.

patterns.title: Trophy bluegill spawn
patterns.technique: Wax worm + small ice jig under foam float; small jigs in spawning beds
patterns.where: Sand + gravel shoreline coves; 3-6 ft of water
patterns.details: Pleasant Lake routinely gives up 10-inch bluegill — the population skews jumbo.

notes: Most famous for trophy bluegill — multiple regional records. Quiet park-style atmosphere.

name: Lake Gage

patterns.title: Spring walleye after ice-out
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/8 oz
patterns.where: North shore points, dropoff edges

notes: Mid-size Steuben lake. Clear, deep, holds quality walleye for its acreage.

name: Big Long Lake

patterns.title: Smallmouth on rock + gravel humps
patterns.technique: Tubes, jerkbaits, drop-shot
patterns.where: Mid-lake humps, points off the deep east basin

notes: Best of the LaGrange County natural lakes for multispecies fishing. Quiet.

name: Witmer Lake

patterns.title: Pad-edge frog bite
patterns.technique: Hollow-body frog, swim jig, weighted plastic frog
patterns.where: Pad fields + grass mats

notes: Compact, character-filled LaGrange County lake with strong largemouth.

name: Skinner Lake

patterns.title: Deep clear-water smallmouth
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned rig, finesse jerkbaits
patterns.where: Deep main-lake structure 18-30 ft

notes: Small but deep — clear water + 75 ft max depth produce excellent smallmouth for the size.

name: Bear Lake

patterns.title: Quality bluegill on submerged timber
patterns.technique: Wax worm + bobber; small jigs
patterns.where: Submerged timber + brush along NE shoreline

notes: Classic Noble County panfish lake.

name: Sylvan Lake (Rome City)
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, walleye, crappie, channel catfish

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR stocked.

species.name: Crappie
species.notes: Sylvan crappie are legitimately big.

patterns.title: Slab crappie in submerged timber
patterns.technique: 1/16-1/8 oz jig + minnow; small swimbaits
patterns.where: Submerged timber + boat docks; 8-15 ft depth in summer
patterns.details: Sylvan is one of the best crappie lakes in NE Indiana.

patterns.title: Bass + walleye on submerged humps
patterns.technique: Carolina rig + crawler harnesses
patterns.where: Mid-lake humps; outside weedline edges

notes: Reservoir-style lake (impounded by an old dam) in Noble County. Heavy timber + multi-species draw.

name: Loon Lake (Noble County)

patterns.title: Smallmouth on deep gravel
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, tubes
patterns.where: Deep gravel humps 20-30 ft

notes: Deep glacial lake — 88 ft max. Quality multi-species for compact acreage.

name: Lake Wawasee

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Top-tier Indiana smallmouth lake.

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: IDNR-stocked premier muskie water.

patterns.title: Deep summer smallmouth
patterns.technique: Drop-shot 4" finesse worm; Ned rig; tubes
patterns.where: Mid-lake humps + rocky points 18-30 ft; bluff edges off the main basin
patterns.details: Wawasee's clear water + cabbage edges make it Indiana's most-celebrated smallmouth lake.

patterns.title: Muskie figure-eight on cabbage edges
patterns.technique: Large bucktails (Cowgirl, Double Cowgirl), gliders, jerkbaits, large topwater
patterns.where: Cabbage + coontail edges; main-lake transitions
patterns.details: Indiana's muskie program has been working on Wawasee since the 1980s — figure-eights at boatside pay regularly.

patterns.title: Walleye on cabbage edges at dusk
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + bottom bouncers; jig + minnow at dusk
patterns.where: Cabbage edges in 10-16 ft; deep points

regulations: Statewide bass + walleye limits. Muskie 36" minimum (consult current IDNR regs).
managementProgram: IDNR muskie stocking program — flagship water
Wawasee Property Owners Association lake management cooperation
notes: Indiana's largest natural lake. The Wawasee + Syracuse + Papakeechie chain holds some of the best multi-species water in the state. Three glacial basins with the deep west basin holding the suspended muskie + walleye.

name: Syracuse Lake

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: Connected to Wawasee — fish move.

patterns.title: Largemouth pad-edge bite
patterns.technique: Frog, senko, swim jig
patterns.where: Pad edges + dock corners

notes: Connected to Wawasee by a channel. Shallower than Wawasee but holds the same species mix at smaller scale.

name: Tippecanoe Lake

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: Premier IDNR muskie water.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Clear deep water = high-end smallmouth.

species.name: Cisco (lake herring)
species.notes: Self-sustaining native population in the deep basin.

patterns.title: Deep summer muskie on cisco forage
patterns.technique: Big rubber (Bull Dawgs), large gliders, big jerkbaits
patterns.where: Deep basin transitions 25-50 ft where cisco school
patterns.details: Tippe muskie key on cisco — this is the rare midwest muskie water where deep open-water trolling produces fish. 50+ inchers documented.

patterns.title: Smallmouth on deep humps
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned, deep tubes
patterns.where: Mid-lake humps 20-35 ft; clear deep points

patterns.title: Cisco ice fishing
patterns.technique: Cisco rigs, small jigging spoons, waxworms
patterns.where: Deep basin 70-100 ft
patterns.details: Some of the only public-access cisco ice fishing in IN.

managementProgram: IDNR muskie stocking program — flagship water
notes: The deepest natural lake in Indiana (123 ft). Combination of cisco forage + deep clear water makes it the state's premier muskie fishery.

name: Webster Lake + Backwater Lake

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: IDNR-stocked.

patterns.title: Cabbage-edge muskie
patterns.technique: Bucktails, gliders, rubber
patterns.where: Cabbage / coontail edges; channel-to-Backwater area

patterns.title: Largemouth in the connecting channel + pads
patterns.technique: Senkos, flipping jigs, frogs
patterns.where: Pad fields in Backwater Lake; channel corners

notes: Webster + Backwater are connected. North Webster is the lake town. Strong multi-species + the muskie program made it a destination.

name: Winona Lake

patterns.title: Smallmouth on deep clear-water structure
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned, tubes
patterns.where: South basin deep points; bluff edges

notes: Adjacent to Warsaw. Glacial basin with deep clear water. Underrated for smallmouth quality.

name: Pike Lake (Warsaw)

species.name: Channel catfish
species.notes: IDNR stocked.

patterns.title: Channel cat slip-bobber
patterns.technique: Cut bait + circle hook; chicken liver
patterns.where: Channel edges; shoreline dropoffs after dark

notes: Compact urban Warsaw lake. Heavily used for shore + small-boat access.

name: Chapman Lake (Big + Little)

patterns.title: Walleye on points + cabbage edges
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses, jig + minnow
patterns.where: Main-lake points; cabbage edges 8-14 ft

notes: Big Chapman + Little Chapman are connected. Less famous than Wawasee or Tippe but high-quality.

name: Barbee Lakes Chain
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, bluegill, channel catfish, muskie

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: IDNR-stocked in select chain lakes.

patterns.title: Pad-and-channel largemouth
patterns.technique: Senkos, flipping jigs, frogs, swim jigs
patterns.where: Pad fields, channel mouths, dock corners across all chain lakes

notes: Seven connected lakes — Big Barbee, Little Barbee, Sawmill, Kuhn, Banning, Irish, Sechrist. Navigation between them via channels. One of the most underrated multi-species systems in Indiana.

name: Tippecanoe River
county: Kosciusko / Fulton / Marshall / Pulaski / White / Tippecanoe / Carroll
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, channel catfish, flathead catfish

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Indiana's premier smallmouth river. Cool clear water + abundant rock + gravel.

species.name: Flathead catfish
species.notes: Trophy class in deeper holes.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Below Lake Shafer / Lake Freeman dams; tailwater fish.

patterns.title: Wade-fishing summer smallmouth
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, in-line spinners, soft jerkbaits, Rapala Shad Raps
patterns.where: Below riffles; rock + gravel bars; current seams; downed timber
patterns.details: The Tippe is the gold standard Indiana wade-fishing smallmouth river. Most-quality stretches: North Manchester to Logansport, plus below Lake Freeman dam.

patterns.title: Float-trip catfish run
patterns.technique: Cut bait + circle hooks; live bluegill for flatheads
patterns.where: Deep holes, log jams, outside bends

patterns.title: Tailwater walleye below Oakdale Dam
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/4 oz; small swimbaits
patterns.where: Lake Freeman / Oakdale Dam tailwater; deep current breaks

regulations: Statewide bass regs. Tippe has stretches with rare-mussel protection — check site-specific rules.
managementProgram: Federally-protected mussel species in upper river
notes: The Tippe is the iconic Indiana smallmouth river — biologists describe it as one of the most biodiverse rivers in the Midwest. Wade or canoe; never a bad time to be on it in summer.

name: Eel River (Northern Indiana)

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Cool gravel runs — wading classic.

patterns.title: Eel River wading smallmouth
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, in-line spinners, surface poppers at dawn
patterns.where: Below riffles; gravel runs; current seams below downed timber
patterns.details: Twin of the Tippecanoe at smaller scale. Less pressured.

notes: Separate from the Southern Indiana Eel River (which flows to the White). This northern Eel is a Wabash trib, draining Whitley + Wabash + Cass counties to Logansport. Premier wadeable smallmouth water.

name: Wabash River (Logansport to Lafayette)
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, flathead catfish, sauger, white bass

species.name: Flathead catfish
species.notes: True trophy class — Wabash is a known flathead destination.

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Fall + winter run below dams.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Spring run is a tradition; schools below dams.

species.name: Hybrid striped bass
species.notes: Stocked into select stretches.

patterns.title: Spring white bass + sauger below dams
patterns.technique: Jigs + curly-tail grubs at 1/4-3/8 oz; small swimbaits; in-line spinners
patterns.where: Below the lock-and-dam structures; current seams; eddy edges
patterns.details: Lafayette area + Logansport tailwaters get the white-bass spring blitz.

patterns.title: Trophy flathead night fishing
patterns.technique: Live bluegill / 6-8 in chub on heavy circle hooks; bait-clicker rigs
patterns.where: Deep outside bends; log jams; bridge piers
patterns.details: Wabash flatheads are legitimate — 40+ pound fish are caught every summer.

patterns.title: Wading smallmouth lower flow
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs
patterns.where: Gravel + rock bars; behind midstream rocks; current breaks

regulations: Statewide regs. Flathead 20+ lb informally treated as trophy class by guides.
notes: Indiana's signature warmwater river. The Wabash carries the state's largest flatheads, a serious white bass spring run, and underrated smallmouth between Logansport and Lafayette.

name: Mississinewa Reservoir (Peru)
signatureSpecies: Muskellunge, walleye, white bass, channel catfish

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: USACE / IDNR cooperative muskie program. Top Indiana muskie reservoir.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Big spring run + summer schooling.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Pre-impoundment river structure + rip-rap shorelines.

patterns.title: Muskie figure-8 on rocky points
patterns.technique: Bucktails, large rubber, gliders, large topwater
patterns.where: Riprap points; flooded timber edges; mouth of Mississinewa River arm
patterns.details: Reservoir muskies orient to forage; chase schooling white bass + shad.

patterns.title: Spring + summer white bass schools
patterns.technique: Casting Jigging Rapalas + small spoons + jigs into busting schools
patterns.where: Main-lake flats + river arm mouth
patterns.details: Watch for surface boils in evening — classic schooling white bass action.

patterns.title: Walleye trolling cranks
patterns.technique: Crankbaits + spinner rigs on lead core or planer boards
patterns.where: Main-lake flats 10-18 ft; old river channel edges

regulations: Muskie 36" minimum (confirm current). Statewide walleye + bass regs.
notes: Built 1967 USACE flood-control reservoir on the Mississinewa River. Largest of the Upper Wabash federal reservoirs.

species.name: Walleye

patterns.where: Shallow cove brush + dock pilings

regulations: Hybrid 17" min, 2/day; check current. Standard bass regs.
notes: Indianapolis's signature suburban reservoir. Hybrid stripers are THE reason to fish Geist — the program produces 8+ pound fish regularly.

regulations: Eagle Creek Park entry fee. Standard IN regs.
notes: Indianapolis's premier public reservoir — Eagle Creek Park manages access. Heavy fishing pressure but the IDNR stocking keeps quality up.

signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, crappie, walleye, channel catfish

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR stocked.

species.name: Hybrid striped bass

name: Cataract Lake (Cagles Mill Reservoir)

name: White River, West Fork (Indianapolis)

species.name: Largemouth bass

species.name: White bass

patterns.title: Wading smallmouth through the city
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, in-line spinners, small swimbaits
patterns.where: Below low-head dams (Broad Ripple, downtown); gravel runs + riprap
patterns.details: The White through Broad Ripple to downtown holds genuinely good smallmouth. The river's recovery from the 1999 contamination event is one of Indiana's environmental success stories.

patterns.where: Deep holes below Broad Ripple Dam, downtown low-heads, deep outside bends

notes: The White's Indianapolis recovery is real — it's the city's best urban smallmouth river. Numerous low-head dams create habitat zones.

name: Fall Creek (Indianapolis)
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, channel catfish, rock bass

patterns.title: Compact wade-fishing for smallmouth

patterns.where: Below riffles; rocky pools through Indianapolis

notes: Geist Reservoir's main tributary above + tailwater below. Small wading smallmouth river through Indy.

name: Cicero Creek + Morse Reservoir Headwaters

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Slack-water sections.

patterns.title: Wade-fishing smallmouth above Morse

name: Wildcat Creek (Lafayette area)
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, rock bass, channel catfish

patterns.where: Riffle-to-pool transitions; gravel + rock structure

notes: Major Tippecanoe River tributary entering at Lafayette. Quality wade smallmouth — one of the best-kept secrets in central IN.

name: Sugar Creek (Crawfordsville / Shades / Turkey Run)

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Slack water above each fishing access.

patterns.title: Sugar Creek wade and float
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned, in-line spinners; topwater poppers at dawn
patterns.where: Through Shades + Turkey Run state parks; below the rapids

notes: Iconic IN scenic + fishing creek. Smallmouth water through one of Indiana's most beautiful canyon systems.

name: Pigeon River (Pigeon River FWA)
signatureSpecies: Brown trout, smallmouth bass, northern pike

species.notes: IDNR-stocked. NE Indiana's premier trout stream.

patterns.title: Brown trout on spring caddis + summer terrestrials

patterns.where: Below shaded sections; deep pools below Pigeon River FWA bridges
patterns.details: PRFWA holds the best public-access trout water in Indiana outside of the Lake Michigan tribs.

patterns.title: Sloughs pike on big spinners

patterns.where: Mongo Ponds, Sloughs, dead-water lakes within PRFWA

notes: Indiana's most legitimate inland trout stream. PRFWA covers 12,000+ acres with multiple river-access sites. The cold-water tribs (Solomon Creek, Little Elkhart) also hold stocked trout.

name: Little Elkhart River

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: IDNR-stocked.

patterns.title: Trout dry-fly through shaded sections
patterns.technique: Caddis dries, hopper-dropper, small Wooly Buggers
patterns.where: Below shaded riffles; deep pools

notes: Tributary to the Elkhart River. Smaller, lightly-pressured trout stream that gets stocked alongside the Pigeon.

name: Solomon Creek

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Spring-fed cold water — supports stocked trout year-round.

patterns.title: Cold-water year-round trout
patterns.technique: Small nymphs, midges, small streamers
patterns.where: Spring-fed runs

notes: Spring-fed creek — among the coldest stream water in Indiana. Holds stocked trout in summer when other IN trout streams warm out.

name: Bass Lake (Starke County)

species.notes: IDNR stocked.

notes: Big shallow Starke County lake. Strong walleye + largemouth combo. Less famous than the Kosciusko lakes but high-quality water.

name: Lake Maxinkuckee (Culver)
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, largemouth bass, walleye, muskie

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR stocked.

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: Limited IDNR stocking.

patterns.details: Maxinkuckee is Indiana's #2 natural-lake size + #3 max depth. Clear deep water = elite smallmouth habitat.

notes: Indiana's Mississinewa upstream of the reservoir is overlooked smallmouth water.

name: Salamonie River (above the reservoir)

patterns.title: Smallmouth wading
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned, spinners

name: Lake Superior (Marquette / Munising)
signatureSpecies: Lake trout, coho salmon, splake, coaster brook trout

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Lake Superior is the lake-trout capital of the Great Lakes. Healthy, self-sustaining populations.

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: Stocked + naturalized. Spring near-shore, summer offshore.

species.name: Chinook salmon
species.notes: Less abundant than Lake Michigan; fall staging at trib mouths.

species.name: Splake (lake×brook hybrid)
species.notes: Stocked specifically for shore + harbor anglers — Superior is the splake capital.

species.name: Coaster brook trout
species.notes: A genuinely unique Lake Superior strain — large brookies that use the lake. Conservation regs.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Trib runs spring + fall.

species.name: Lake whitefish
species.notes: Commercially-important, anglers ice-fish for them.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Rocky shoreline summer.

patterns.title: Lake trout summer trolling
patterns.technique: Downrigger or wire-line trolling with spoons (Sutton 44, NK28) + dodger-and-flies; jigging tubes + Swedish Pimples over reefs
patterns.where: 60-180 ft of water; structure-oriented reefs like Stannard Rock, Granite Island, Big Reef
patterns.details: Most-targeted Superior fish. Stannard Rock (south of Keweenaw) is a destination reef — anglers boat from Marquette + Big Bay. Trolling speed 1.8-2.4 mph. Match the local smelt and cisco forage.

patterns.title: Spring shoreline coho + splake
patterns.technique: Casting Little Cleos + Krocodile spoons (1/2-3/4 oz) + Husky Jerks from shore; trolling within 1/4 mi of shore for splake
patterns.where: Harbor breakwaters (Marquette, Munising, Big Bay), pier mouths, river-mouth current seams
patterns.details: Stained run-off concentrates fish along the south shore. Splake key off harbor structure — Munising Bay + Marquette's lower harbor are reliable.

patterns.title: Fall salmon at trib mouths
patterns.technique: Casting spoons + spinners + Cleos from piers; bobbers with skein at river mouths
patterns.where: Carp River (Marquette), Anna River (Munising), Two Hearted, Big Garlic mouths
patterns.details: Less concentrated than Lake Michigan tribs but quality. Skamania-strain steelhead also begin staging.

patterns.title: Coaster brook trout shoreline
patterns.technique: Small streamers swung from shore; light-tackle spinners (size 1-2 Mepps); fly: black-nose dace, sculpins
patterns.where: Rocky points off the Keweenaw + Pictured Rocks shoreline; near-shore boulder fields
patterns.details: Coasters are a once-in-a-decade fish for most anglers. Keep them wet — these are the genuine native Lake Superior strain. Salazar Strict regs apply.

patterns.title: Whitefish ice-fishing
patterns.technique: Small jigging spoons + waxworms or mousie grubs; tip-downs with single egg
patterns.where: Big Bay de Noc + offshore over reefs in 40-80 ft
patterns.details: Whitefish are wary and finicky — small jigs in chartreuse + green. Big Bay (north end of Bay de Noc) is the heart of the UP whitefish fishery.

notes: The world's largest freshwater lake by surface area sits on Michigan's northern coast. Stannard Rock, Granite Island, and Big Reef are pilgrimage-class lake-trout destinations. Cold + clear + deep — fishery feels like saltwater.

name: Lake Superior (Keweenaw Bay + Bays)

species.name: Smelt
species.notes: Spring dipping run April-May in tribs.

patterns.title: Keweenaw Bay laker trolling
patterns.technique: Wire-line + downriggers; spoons + cowbell-and-fly rigs
patterns.where: Off Houghton + L'Anse; 80-200 ft over structure
patterns.details: Keweenaw Bay is one of the most reliable laker boxes in the Great Lakes. Charter scene is robust.

patterns.title: Spring smelt dipping
patterns.technique: Long-handled smelt nets in tributary streams during the spawn run
patterns.where: Sturgeon River, Otter River, Silver River, smaller Keweenaw tribs
patterns.details: A UP cultural tradition. Run lasts ~2 weeks; fish move at night.

notes: The Keweenaw Peninsula extends 70 mi into Superior. Eagle Harbor + Copper Harbor are the iconic far-north access points.

name: Big Two Hearted River

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: The Hemingway river. Wild brookies up the whole system.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Spring + fall runs from Lake Superior.

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: Fall run.

species.name: Northern pike
species.notes: Lower river sloughs.

patterns.title: Wild brookie wading
patterns.technique: Small dries (Adams, Royal Wulff, Madsen's Skunk #14-16); small streamers (Mickey Finn, Wooly Bugger size 8-10)
patterns.where: Below shaded pools, log jams, undercut banks — wade or canoe
patterns.details: The Two Hearted is the quintessential UP brookie river. Hemingway wrote "Big Two-Hearted River" about a sister stream (actually the Fox), but the name made this one famous. Pristine, lightly pressured outside July.

patterns.title: Spring + fall steelhead
patterns.technique: Egg patterns, yarn flies, small streamers under indicators; spawn sacs
patterns.where: Deep pools below riffles; the lower river to the mouth
patterns.details: Less crowded than Lake Michigan tribs. Skamania-strain + winter strain.

regulations: Designated Blue Ribbon trout stream. Bait restrictions in upper stretches.
notes: Lightly pressured. Wilderness character — most access requires gravel-road driving. Wade-and-camp culture.

name: Fox River (UP)

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Hemingway's actual "Big Two-Hearted River" — he changed the name in the story.

patterns.title: Hemingway-style brookie wading
patterns.technique: Small dries (#14-18 Adams, Royal Wulff); brookie streamers
patterns.where: Below the Seney Wildlife Refuge boundary; pocket water + small pools
patterns.details: Quiet wading classic. Seney WR controls headwaters access.

notes: The actual river behind Hemingway's "Big Two-Hearted River." Pristine UP brookie water.

name: Tahquamenon River
signatureSpecies: Muskellunge, walleye, northern pike, smallmouth bass

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: Genuine UP musky river — tannin-dark water + slow current.

patterns.title: Tannin-water musky
patterns.technique: Big bucktails, gliders, large rubber (Bull Dawg); slow retrieves in dark water
patterns.where: Lower river below the Upper + Lower Falls; deep pools + downed timber
patterns.details: Tea-stained tannin water from the cedar swamp headwaters. Visibility is short — fish key on vibration. Black + chartreuse colors dominate.

patterns.title: Lower-river walleye + pike
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/8-1/4 oz; large in-line spinners
patterns.where: Below Lower Falls to mouth at Whitefish Bay; deep tannin pools

notes: Famous for the falls but also genuinely good musky water. The tannin-stained water from cedar headwaters is a Hoosier rarity — fish hold differently in dark water.

name: Sturgeon River (UP)

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Spring + fall runs.

species.name: Smelt
species.notes: Spring spawn run from Keweenaw Bay.

patterns.title: Brookie + brown summer pocket water
patterns.technique: Small dries + hopper-dropper; small streamers (Wooly Bugger, sculpin patterns)
patterns.where: Pocket water above the lower river; rocky riffles

patterns.title: Spring smelt-dipping run
patterns.technique: Long-handled smelt nets
patterns.where: Lower river up from the mouth
patterns.details: UP cultural tradition. Smelt run dense — fill 5-gallon buckets.

notes: Sturgeon Gorge wilderness in the upper river is a dramatic high-walled canyon. Confusingly named — there are multiple "Sturgeon Rivers" in MI.

name: Manistique River
signatureSpecies: Muskellunge, walleye, smallmouth bass, brown trout

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: Naturalized population — one of the best wild musky rivers in MI.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Upper river + tributaries.

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: Fall run up from Lake Michigan.

patterns.title: Wild-musky river fishing
patterns.technique: Bucktails + glides; large rubber over downed timber
patterns.where: Below Manistique Falls; deep slow stretches with timber
patterns.details: The Manistique gives up legitimate trophy musky to the patient angler. Less pressured than the LP musky scene.

patterns.title: Float-trip smallmouth
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, in-line spinners
patterns.where: Below Indian Lake; gravel + rock structure

notes: Drains the largest watershed in the UP. Holds wild musky + walleye + bass — the rare "everything" river.

name: Lake Gogebic
signatureSpecies: Walleye, smallmouth bass, perch, northern pike

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Largest natural lake in the UP. Strong wild walleye fishery.

species.name: Northern pike
species.notes: Trophy class in cabbage flats.

patterns.title: Summer walleye on cabbage edges
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + bottom bouncers; jig + leech 1/8 oz; trolling Reef Runners at dusk
patterns.where: Cabbage edges 12-18 ft; classic UP weedline pattern
patterns.details: Gogebic walleye orient to cabbage all summer. Evening + night dominate. Heavy lake — eats lures.

patterns.title: Big-pike weedline
patterns.technique: Large in-line spinners; jerkbaits; suckers for trophy class
patterns.where: Outside cabbage edges; transitions to deeper water

patterns.title: Winter perch + walleye
patterns.technique: Jigging spoons + waxworms; tip-downs with minnows
patterns.where: Mid-lake basin; main-lake humps
patterns.details: UP ice culture is real — Gogebic gets dotted with shanties.

notes: The UP's largest natural lake — 14 miles long. Stretches across the IronwoodMichigan and Wisconsin border (actually entirely in MI but very close to WI).

name: Indian Lake (Schoolcraft)

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: Connected to Manistique River musky system.

patterns.title: Crawler harness walleye
patterns.technique: Bottom bouncers + crawler harnesses
patterns.where: Mid-lake flats 8-14 ft

notes: Fourth-largest inland lake in MI (and largest in the UP after Gogebic). Feeds the Manistique River — fish move between systems.

name: Big Bay de Noc (Lake Michigan)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Big Bay de Noc is the most reliable walleye box on northern Lake Michigan.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Trophy-class smallmouth — Big Bay was a longstanding bass-tournament destination.

species.name: Lake whitefish
species.notes: Ice fishery north end.

patterns.title: Smallmouth on rocky points + reefs
patterns.technique: Drop-shot 4-in finesse worm; Ned rigs; tubes; jerkbaits
patterns.where: Rocky reefs + points + drop-offs 12-25 ft
patterns.details: Big Bay's clear water + abundant reefs = trophy smallmouth habitat. Fish often suspend over deeper structure feeding on alewives.

patterns.title: Walleye trolling main-lake structure
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses on lead-core; Reef Runners + Husky Jerks
patterns.where: Main-bay flats 15-25 ft

patterns.title: Whitefish ice fishing
patterns.technique: Small jigging spoons + waxworms or mousie grubs
patterns.where: North end (Garden Peninsula side) in 40-80 ft

notes: Half of the famed Bays de Noc system. Connected to Little Bay de Noc to the south. Walleye + smallmouth power-water.

name: Little Bay de Noc (Lake Michigan)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: World-class walleye — Little Bay de Noc gives up double-digit fish every year.

patterns.title: Trophy walleye trolling
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses on bottom bouncers; trolling Reef Runners + Husky Jerks; jig + minnow on shallow flats spring/fall
patterns.where: Channel edges; the Ford River mouth; main basin 12-30 ft
patterns.details: Spring walleye stage in the Ford River mouth area. Summer fish suspend on the main basin. Big fish (8-12 lb) are caught here regularly.

patterns.title: Smallmouth on river-mouth structure
patterns.technique: Tubes, drop-shot, Ned
patterns.where: Rocky points off Escanaba + Gladstone

notes: Smaller, more enclosed than Big Bay. Considered one of the best Lake Michigan walleye fisheries in the entire Great Lakes basin.

name: Big Manistee River (Tippy Dam to Lake Michigan)

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run mid-Aug through October. Tippy Dam tailwater holds them deep into the season.

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: Run alongside kings.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Skamania summer-run + winter strain. Year-round fishery thanks to Tippy tailwater.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Trophy-class browns key off the salmon eggs in fall. World-class night fishery during Hex.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Tippy tailwater + lower river.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Summer residential below the dam.

species.name: Lake sturgeon
species.notes: Reintroduction; catch + release only.

patterns.title: Tippy tailwater fall king salmon
patterns.technique: Float-fishing skein + spawn sacs; large spinners; spey casting big streamers; back-bouncing eggs in deep tailwater
patterns.where: Below Tippy Dam down to High Bridge + Bear Creek; deep gravel runs; the holes off the powerhouse
patterns.details: Tippy is the most-fished salmon hole in the Great Lakes. Crowds in October — go weekday early. 12-15 lb mainline, 10 lb fluoro leader. Eggs in chartreuse / oregon-cheese / clown colors.

patterns.title: Night Hex brown trout
patterns.when: Late June through mid-July, 65-72°F water, dark + warm + humid nights
patterns.technique: Size 6 Hex dries (foam-back + parachute variants); large dry-fly setups; 1x-2x leader strong enough for 20+ inch browns
patterns.where: Below the dam down through Bear Creek; quiet slow water with silt-bottom Hex emergence
patterns.details: The Hex is the highlight of MI fly fishing. Big browns rise to size 6 mayflies in the dark. Drive the river to scout silt-bottom holes by daylight. Headlamp on red filter while fishing — white light spooks. Fish 9 PM until midnight.

patterns.title: Winter + spring steelhead
patterns.technique: Float-rig with spawn sacs / yarn flies / sucker spawn; nymphing Hex nymphs + stones + eggs; swinging Intruder + Stu's Ostrich Mini-Intruder
patterns.where: Below Tippy down to High Bridge + Bear Creek; deep pool tail-outs
patterns.details: Tippy tailwater holds steelhead year-round because the dam discharges cold water. Pink + chartreuse stand out in stained winter water. Skamania-strain summer run starts in July.

patterns.title: Articulated streamer trophy browns
patterns.technique: Galloup's Sex Dungeon, Boogie Man, Drunk & Disorderly, Triple D; Lynch's Mini D&D in lower water
patterns.where: Below the dam + the lower river; deep banks + holes
patterns.details: Trophy MI brown program. Fall fish key on salmon parr + bigger forage. Strip with stop-and-go pulls — pause is when most eats come.

regulations: MI trout/salmon license + stamp. Specific gear regs (no-bait sections, hook restrictions) — confirm current.
managementProgram: MI DNR Lake Michigan salmonid stocking
Consumers Energy Tippy + Hodenpyl dam licensing
notes: Tippy Dam is the focal point of Michigan steelhead + salmon culture. Lower river (Tippy to mouth) is the trophy zone — the upper Manistee above Hodenpyl is a separate world (see Upper Manistee entry).

name: Upper Manistee River

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Wild MI brown stronghold. Trophy class possible in the lower flies-only section.

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Native, wild fish in the upper reaches.

patterns.title: Hex hatch night browns
patterns.technique: Size 6 Hex dries + emergers; spinners falls at dusk
patterns.where: Silt-bottom slow water; the flies-only section + below CCC bridge
patterns.details: Same Hex window as the lower Big Manistee but with smaller-water character. The flies-only stretches concentrate the high-quality wild browns.

patterns.title: Borchers Drake + Roberts Yellow Drake hatches
patterns.technique: Borchers Drake parachute (size 12-14), Roberts Yellow Drake (size 8-10), Adams parachute
patterns.where: Throughout the upper river; classic MI dry-fly water
patterns.details: Borchers Drake (Ernie Borchers, MI) imitates a wide range of mayflies. Roberts Yellow Drake (Clarence Roberts, Onaway MI) is one of the great MI searching dries.

patterns.title: Streamer-fishing wild browns
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers (Sex Dungeon, Mini D&D, Circus Peanut, Madonna)
patterns.where: Deep banks + log jams + the flies-only zone
patterns.details: The Galloup streamer revolution was born on this river. Strip with pause. Fall fish are the largest.

regulations: Flies-only stretches with strict gear regs. Wild-trout management — bag limits + minimum sizes higher than statewide.
managementProgram: MI DNR wild brown trout management
notes: The Upper Manistee + Au Sable Holy Water are the heart of MI dry-fly culture. Wading + canoeing wild-trout water with deep traditions (Gates Au Sable Lodge, Schmidt's Outfitters, etc.).

name: Pere Marquette River

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Genuinely wild + self-sustaining population — first MI river to get them.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Wild population. Hex hatch + streamer fishery.

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Upper tributaries.

patterns.title: Fall king salmon — flies-only section
patterns.technique: Indicator nymphing with eggs + Hex nymphs; swinging streamers; bobber-dogging on the gear-allowed sections
patterns.where: M-37 to Walhalla (flies-only); below Walhalla (gear allowed)
patterns.details: The PM was the first MI river stocked with king salmon (1960s). Self-sustaining now. The flies-only stretch from M-37 to Gleason's Landing is one of the most-fished fly-only salmon waters in the country.

patterns.title: Night Hex brown trout
patterns.technique: Size 6 Hex dries + emergers
patterns.where: Silt-bottom holes below the M-37 bridge + Baldwin area
patterns.details: PM Hex is celebrated. Big browns rise. Headlamp on red. Often called the most challenging Hex water in MI due to selective fish.

patterns.title: Steelhead — float + swing
patterns.technique: Float-rig spawn / yarn / sucker spawn; swing Intruders + small Spey flies
patterns.where: Below M-37 to Walhalla; deep pool tail-outs
patterns.details: Skamania + winter strain. Less crowded than the Manistee but quality fishing.

regulations: Flies-only stretch M-37 to Gleason's. Specific tippet + hook regs.
managementProgram: MI DNR salmon stocking (historical foundation site)
notes: Baldwin is the PM's lodge town. Schmidt's Outfitters is here — the modern streamer school is rooted in this river.

name: Au Sable River — Holy Water + South Branch
signatureSpecies: Brown trout, brook trout, rainbow trout

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Wild population. Heart of MI dry-fly culture.

patterns.title: Hex hatch night fishing
patterns.technique: Size 6 Hex dries (Roberts Yellow Drake, Robert's Drake, foam-back Hex); spinners
patterns.where: Below Mio + Wakeley Bridge area; silt-bottom slow stretches
patterns.details: The Holy Water Hex is the most-loved MI hatch event. 9 PM-midnight. Headlamp on red. Crowds in peak weeks — get spot early.

patterns.title: Borchers + Roberts dry-fly fishing
patterns.technique: Borchers Drake (size 12-14), Roberts Yellow Drake (size 8-12), Madsen's Skunk, Adams parachute
patterns.where: Holy Water — Burton's Landing to Wakeley Bridge; classic MI dry-fly water
patterns.details: Rusty Gates + Roberts patterns dominate. The Au Sable canon — Rusty's Spinner, Bear's Mine Sweeper. Fish drys all summer.

patterns.title: Big-Three hatches (Hex + Sulfur + Iso)
patterns.technique: Match-the-hatch dries + emergers + spinner falls
patterns.where: Throughout the Holy Water
patterns.details: Au Sable mayfly diversity is unmatched in MI. Iso (Isonychia) brings big browns up in August. Pseudocloeon BWO in fall extends the season.

patterns.title: Streamer-fishing wild trout
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers; smaller patterns than the Manistee (Mini D&D, Cone Head Bunny, Bunny Wabbit)
patterns.where: Below Mio Dam + downstream; banks + log jams
patterns.details: Tighter water than the Manistee — downsized streamers work better.

regulations: Flies-only sections (Holy Water). Catch-and-release for wild fish in select stretches. Bait restrictions.
managementProgram: TU + Gates Au Sable Lodge stewardship
notes: Trout Unlimited was founded on the Au Sable in 1959. Gates Au Sable Lodge is the cultural anchor. The Holy Water (8 miles below Burton's) is one of the most-celebrated fly-fishing stretches in North America.

name: Boardman River

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Wild + stocked. Post-dam-removal restoration improving habitat.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Run from Grand Traverse Bay (Lake Michigan).

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Upper river + tributaries.

patterns.title: Hex hatch night browns
patterns.technique: Size 6 Hex dries
patterns.where: Silt-bottom holes below Brown Bridge

patterns.title: Steelhead winter + spring
patterns.technique: Float-rig with spawn / yarn flies
patterns.where: Below the dam removal sites; lower river to Boardman Lake
patterns.details: Dam removal restored 30+ miles of fish passage to Grand Traverse Bay. Steelhead numbers improving year-over-year.

notes: Three dam removals (2012-2017) restored the Boardman to free-flowing. Fishery is in active recovery — getting better every year.

name: Betsie River

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Strong fall run.

patterns.title: Fall salmon at Homestead Dam
patterns.technique: Float-rig with eggs + yarn flies; spinners; spey casting
patterns.where: Homestead Dam tailwater; downstream pools to the mouth
patterns.details: Homestead Dam is the upstream limit — fish stack below it. Crowded in peak weekends, quieter weekdays.

patterns.title: Steelhead in winter
patterns.technique: Float-rig with spawn sacs + yarn flies; nymphing
patterns.where: Pools below Homestead Dam

notes: Smaller and more intimate than the Manistee. Frankfort is the harbor town at the mouth.

name: Platte River

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: The Platte was the BIRTHPLACE of Great Lakes coho stocking (1966). Still a key coho river.

patterns.title: Coho run — Platte River weir
patterns.technique: Float-rig with spawn sacs + eggs; spinners + small spoons
patterns.where: Below the Platte River weir downstream to Lake Michigan; Veterans Memorial State Forest
patterns.details: The Platte gives up more cohos per mile than any other MI river in peak season. Crowds for the run — Veterans Memorial campground fills up.

managementProgram: MI DNR coho salmon program (foundation site, 1966)
notes: Birthplace of the Great Lakes coho salmon fishery. The MI DNR stocked the first cohos here in 1966 — the entire Great Lakes salmon fishery cascades from this river.

name: Muskegon River (Croton Dam to Lake)
signatureSpecies: King salmon, steelhead, brown trout, walleye

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Genuine year-round fishery thanks to cold dam tailwater.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Strong tailwater fishery + lower river to Muskegon Lake.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Summer residential.

patterns.title: Croton tailwater year-round steelhead
patterns.technique: Indicator nymphing with eggs + Hex nymphs + small stones; swinging Intruders; bobber-dogging
patterns.where: Croton Dam down to Newaygo; deep pool tail-outs + gravel runs
patterns.details: Cold-water dam releases keep steelhead in even in summer. Skamania-strain + winter strain.

patterns.title: Walleye + brown trout below Croton
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/8-1/4 oz; small swimbaits; spring tube jig walleye
patterns.where: Croton tailwater + downstream to Newaygo
patterns.details: Croton tailwater walleye are big — 20+ inch fish caught regularly in spring + fall. Sharing the same water as steelhead + browns.

patterns.title: Trophy brown trout streamer fishing
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers (Sex Dungeon, Boogie Man, Triple D)
patterns.where: Banks + log jams below the dam

managementProgram: Consumers Energy Croton + Hardy + Rogers dams
MI DNR Lake Michigan salmonid stocking
notes: The Muskegon is the longest fishable steelhead tailwater in MI. The cold dam discharges create exceptional year-round conditions. Croton tailwater is a destination for serious steelheaders.

name: White River (Hesperia to Lake Michigan)

patterns.title: Salmon at Hesperia Dam
patterns.technique: Float-rig with eggs; spinners
patterns.where: Hesperia Dam tailwater; downstream to White Lake

notes: Less famous than its sister rivers but quality fishery. Smaller water — more intimate.

name: Little Manistee River

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Famous for being the MI DNR egg-take river — the steelhead strain that stocks the rest of the Great Lakes originates here.

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Upper river.

patterns.title: Spring steelhead — small water
patterns.technique: Float-rig with spawn sacs; nymphing small flies; light tackle for the smaller river
patterns.where: Below Stronach Dam; M-55 access
patterns.details: The Little Manistee is smaller than the Big Manistee — more intimate. Steelhead are the same fish that get egg-stripped at the MI DNR weir to seed the Great Lakes.

managementProgram: MI DNR Little Manistee egg-take weir (steelhead strain source for Great Lakes)
notes: The MI DNR's steelhead egg-take weir is here — the strain stocked into other rivers (and into Lake Superior tribs) originates from these fish.

name: Rifle River

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Lake Huron strain — different from the Lake Michigan tribs.

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Upper river.

patterns.title: Lake Huron strain steelhead
patterns.technique: Float-rig with spawn sacs + yarn flies
patterns.where: Below Selkirk Dam; lower river
patterns.details: Lake Huron strain — chrome bright. Less crowded than Lake Michigan tribs.

notes: Premier Lake Huron tributary on the LP's east side. Less famous than Lake Michigan rivers but quality.

name: Higgins Lake
signatureSpecies: Lake trout, smallmouth bass, perch, walleye

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: One of the few inland MI lakes with a self-sustaining laker population.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Clear deep water = trophy smallmouth.

species.name: Cisco (lake herring)
species.notes: Native population, deep basin.

species.name: Whitefish
species.notes: Ice fishery.

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: IDNR stocked.

patterns.title: Summer lake trout jigging
patterns.technique: Swedish Pimples + Slender Spoons + tube jigs in white/glow; vertical jigging over deep structure
patterns.where: Deep basin 60-120 ft; main-lake humps
patterns.details: Higgins lakers are deep + finicky. Match the cisco forage. Set up over electronics-marked structure.

patterns.title: Smallmouth on deep clear-water structure
patterns.technique: Drop-shot 4-in finesse worm; Ned rigs; deep tubes; finesse jerkbaits
patterns.where: Rocky points + humps 18-30 ft; the deeper drops on the south basin
patterns.details: Higgins is the gold-standard MI inland-lake smallmouth water — clear deep glacial basin. Light fluoro mandatory.

patterns.title: Cisco + whitefish ice fishing
patterns.technique: Small jigging spoons + waxworms; cisco rigs
patterns.where: Deep basin 80-130 ft
patterns.details: Higgins is one of the few public-access cisco ice fisheries in MI. Bobber-style + glow jigs in dark deep water.

managementProgram: MI DNR lake trout management (one of few inland populations)
notes: One of the clearest, deepest inland lakes in MI. Glacial origin. The high-end smallmouth + lake-trout combo is rare outside of Lake Michigan.

name: Houghton Lake

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR-stocked. Strong fishery despite shallow water.

species.name: Northern pike
species.notes: Big pike in the weed flats.

patterns.title: Shallow weed-flat walleye
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + 1/4 oz bottom bouncers; jig + minnow on weed edges; troll #5-7 Husky Jerks at dusk
patterns.where: Weed edges around the entire shoreline; 8-14 ft transitions
patterns.details: Houghton is the largest inland lake in MI. Shallow throughout (avg 8 ft) so walleye are weedline-oriented year-round. Night bite peaks May + September.

patterns.title: Tip-up pike ice fishing
patterns.technique: Tip-ups with large shiners or smelt; jigging large spoons
patterns.where: Cabbage edges; weed flats; channel edges
patterns.details: Houghton ice shanty culture is the largest in MI. "Tip-Up Town" festival in January.

patterns.title: Spring crappie + bluegill
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under bobber; small jigs in pinkish-white colors
patterns.where: Spawning flats; coves + canals

notes: MI's largest inland lake at 20,044 acres. Houghton + Higgins are sometimes called "the Big Two" of central MI lakes. Houghton is the shallow + weedy + multi-species sister to deep + clear Higgins.

name: Burt Lake

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Strong wild + stocked population.

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: IDNR-stocked + naturalized.

species.name: Cisco
species.notes: Native population.

patterns.title: Inland Waterway musky
patterns.technique: Big bucktails (Cowgirl, Double Cowgirl), gliders, large rubber (Bull Dawgs)
patterns.where: Cabbage edges; the channels to Mullett + Crooked Lake
patterns.details: Burt + Mullett + Crooked + Pickerel are the "Inland Waterway" — connected via channels. Musky move between systems.

patterns.title: Walleye trolling cabbage edges
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + bottom bouncers; jig + minnow at dusk
patterns.where: Cabbage edges 10-18 ft

patterns.title: Smallmouth on deep structure
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned, tubes
patterns.where: Rocky points + humps; transition to the deep basin

notes: Part of the Inland Waterway connecting Burt + Mullett + Crooked + Pickerel lakes via the Crooked + Indian Rivers. Premier multi-species lake.

name: Mullett Lake
signatureSpecies: Walleye, smallmouth bass, muskie, lake trout

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Mullett gives up some of the largest inland smallmouth in MI.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Self-sustaining population in the deep basin (148 ft max).

patterns.title: Deep lake trout jigging
patterns.technique: White tube jigs + Swedish Pimples; jigging spoons in 80-130 ft
patterns.where: Deep main basin (deepest inland lake in LP)
patterns.details: Mullett is one of two inland MI lakes deep enough for lake trout (Torch is the other). Native cisco forage drives the laker population.

patterns.title: Trophy smallmouth
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned, deep tubes; jerkbaits in spring
patterns.where: Deep rocky points; transitions to the main basin

notes: Third-largest inland lake in MI. The deepest at 148 ft. Part of the Inland Waterway connecting to Burt + Crooked + Pickerel.

name: Black Lake

species.name: Lake sturgeon
species.notes: Black Lake is the only place in MI you can target sturgeon — and only during the brief February spear-fishing season.

patterns.title: February sturgeon spear-fishing
patterns.when: February (5-day season, max 6 fish caught lake-wide)
patterns.technique: Spear-fishing through ice with decoys + sucker bait
patterns.where: Permitted area near the Onaway end
patterns.details: The MI DNR sets a 5-day spearing season with a 6-fish lake-wide quota. Once the quota is hit, the season closes — sometimes within hours of opening. Anglers wait in shanties for hours. Iconic MI cultural event.

patterns.title: Walleye summer trolling
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses; deep-diving cranks
patterns.where: Main-lake flats

regulations: Sturgeon season is a 5-day spear-only February event with strict permit + quota. Catch-and-release sturgeon angling year-round (no harvest outside the season).
managementProgram: MI DNR sturgeon recovery + spear season
notes: The ONLY place in MI where sturgeon can be harvested (and only during the 5-day February spear season). MI's greatest fish-cultural event.

name: Lake Charlevoix
signatureSpecies: Lake trout, smallmouth bass, walleye, brown trout

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Naturalized + stocked. Deep basin holds quality fish.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Trophy class on rocky points + drop-offs.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Salmon-program fish that runs up from Lake Michigan via the Pine River channel.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Charlevoix tribs.

species.name: Coho salmon
species.notes: Fall stagers.

patterns.title: Lake trout summer trolling
patterns.technique: Downriggers with spoons; cowbell + fly rigs in deeper water
patterns.where: Main basin 80-120 ft

patterns.title: Trophy smallmouth on deep structure
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned, deep tubes
patterns.where: Rocky points off Boyne City + Horton Bay

managementProgram: MI DNR Lake Michigan salmonid stocking (connected via Pine River channel)
notes: Connected to Lake Michigan via the Pine River channel — Charlevoix is technically an arm of Lake Michigan in many seasons. Big-water character.

name: Torch Lake

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Deepest inland lake in MI = best inland laker fishery.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: IDNR-stocked.

species.name: Cisco
species.notes: Native + abundant; key forage for lakers.

patterns.title: Deep summer laker
patterns.technique: White + glow tube jigs vertical; downrigger trolling with cowbells in deeper water
patterns.where: Mid-lake basin (60-150 ft); deep humps
patterns.details: Torch is the deepest inland lake in MI (285 ft) — laker habitat at inland scale. Clear water + cisco forage = quality fish.

patterns.title: Smallmouth on deep clear water
patterns.technique: Drop-shot 4-in finesse worm; Ned rigs
patterns.where: Rocky points + humps 18-35 ft

managementProgram: MI DNR lake trout + brown trout stocking
notes: Second-clearest lake in MI (after Crystal). Glacial origin, 19 miles long, 285 ft max. Iconic clear-water destination.

name: Walloon Lake

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: The "Hemingway lake" — he wrote about fishing it.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Stocked; deep enough for them.

patterns.title: Smallmouth on deep clear structure
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned, tubes
patterns.where: Mid-lake humps; rocky points off North Arm

notes: Where Hemingway spent his summers — the Nick Adams stories are set on its tributaries. Deep + clear smallmouth water.

name: Crystal Lake (Benzie County)

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: IDNR-stocked. Clear glacial basin = quality fishery.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: IDNR-stocked.

patterns.title: Lake trout in clear water
patterns.technique: Vertical jigging white tubes + Pimples; trolling deep cowbells in summer
patterns.where: Main basin 80-150 ft

notes: The clearest lake in MI. Glacial origin, 8 miles long, 165 ft max. Visibility regularly exceeds 30 ft.

name: Glen Lake (North + South Bay)

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Stocked.

patterns.title: Clear-water smallmouth
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned, finesse jerkbaits
patterns.where: Rocky points + humps

notes: Right next to Sleeping Bear Dunes National Lakeshore. Two connected bays: North (smaller, deeper) + Big Glen (larger, shallower).

name: Hubbard Lake
signatureSpecies: Walleye, smallmouth bass, lake trout, perch

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Strong wild population.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Stocked.

patterns.title: Walleye on main-lake structure
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses; jig + minnow
patterns.where: Mid-lake humps + cabbage edges

notes: NE LP's biggest inland lake. Underrated walleye + smallmouth combo. Quieter than the Inland Waterway lakes.

name: Hamlin Lake

patterns.title: Largemouth on weed flats
patterns.technique: Senkos, frogs, swim jigs
patterns.where: Pad fields + cabbage edges

notes: Adjacent to Ludington State Park. Multi-basin lake — north end is shallower + weedy (largemouth), south end deeper (smallmouth + walleye).

name: Lake Cadillac + Lake Mitchell

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR-stocked.

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: IDNR-stocked. Quality musky fishery for the size.

patterns.title: Connected-lake musky
patterns.technique: Bucktails, gliders, large rubber
patterns.where: Cabbage edges on both lakes; the canal between them
patterns.details: Cadillac + Mitchell are connected via canal. Musky move between systems.

notes: Two connected lakes in the city of Cadillac. Mitchell is the bigger of the two. Quality multi-species fishery.

name: Lake St. Clair
signatureSpecies: Muskellunge, smallmouth bass, walleye, yellow perch

species.name: Muskellunge
species.notes: Arguably the best musky water in North America. Naturalized self-sustaining population. Charters book a year out.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: World-class — Bassmaster Elite tour stops here. The Mile Roads in summer hold trophy bags.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Best near the Detroit River channel + spring spawn run.

species.name: Yellow perch
species.notes: Cyclic but historically epic when on.

patterns.title: Trolling musky on the open lake
patterns.technique: Trolling big crankbaits (Believer, Bull Dawg, 10-12" Grandma) on planer boards; figure-8 boatside on every cast
patterns.where: Mile Roads off St. Clair Shores; the channels off the St. Clair Flats; Anchor Bay weed edges
patterns.details: Lake St. Clair musky population is THE reason this lake is famous. Average fish 36-44 in; 50+ inchers caught every year. Charter scene is robust. Trolling speed 4-6 mph (faster than inland-lake casting).

patterns.title: Drop-shot trophy smallmouth
patterns.technique: Drop-shot 4-in finesse worm; tube jigs; Ned rigs in 10-25 ft
patterns.where: The Mile Roads (12 Mile, 13 Mile, etc.) — submerged road beds off St. Clair Shores; main-lake humps
patterns.details: Lake St. Clair smallmouth fishery is genuinely world-class. Bassmaster Elite + Major League Fishing host events here. Five-fish bags of 5+ lb fish each happen regularly. Wind direction dictates which side fishes — fish into the wind on shore.

patterns.title: Spring walleye + jumbo perch run
patterns.technique: Jigging shiners + minnows; small swimbaits
patterns.where: Off the Detroit River channel; the St. Clair Flats
patterns.details: Walleye stage in the Detroit + St. Clair Rivers in early spring. Perch peak May-June in the open lake.

patterns.title: Anchor Bay largemouth + pike
patterns.technique: Senkos, swim jigs, frogs over pads; in-line spinners + jerkbaits for pike
patterns.where: Anchor Bay (NW corner) — heavy weeds + pads
patterns.details: Anchor Bay is the shallow weed-flat sister to the open lake. Largemouth water at scale.

patterns.title: Winter perch ice fishing
patterns.technique: Jigging minnow + waxworm rigs; tip-downs
patterns.where: Off Selfridge + the Mile Roads
patterns.details: Lake St. Clair ice scene is huge. Perch quality varies cyclically.

regulations: MI license valid; near international waters in spots. Specific musky regs (36" min). Bass tournament regs may apply.
notes: The undisputed #1 musky water in North America. Smallmouth fishery is also world-class. Charter scene is full-time. The Mile Roads are submerged road beds (built before the lake flooded them) that create perfect structure.

name: Detroit River
signatureSpecies: Walleye, smallmouth bass, white bass, lake sturgeon

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: World-class spring walleye run. The Detroit River walleye run is one of the most-fished events in the Great Lakes.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Spring run.

species.name: Lake sturgeon
species.notes: Catch + release. Below Belle Isle.

patterns.title: Spring walleye run
patterns.when: March-May (peak last week March through early May)
patterns.technique: Vertical jigging with 3/8-3/4 oz jigheads + plastic minnows (gulp, swim minnows); hair jigs with stinger hooks
patterns.where: Trenton Channel; off Lake Erie Metropark; below Wyandotte; Belle Isle area; the Livingstone Channel
patterns.details: THE Great Lakes walleye event. Tens of thousands of fish stack in the river to spawn in March-April. Jig vertically in 30-60 ft. Hire a charter — local knowledge matters.

patterns.title: Trophy white bass spring schools
patterns.technique: Casting small jigs + spinners + Cleos into schooling fish
patterns.where: Trenton Channel + off Wyandotte

regulations: Spring walleye special regulations may apply. Sturgeon catch + release only. Border with Canada — US license OK in US waters.
managementProgram: MI DNR + Ontario MNRF cooperative walleye management
notes: The connector between Lake St. Clair and Lake Erie. Spring walleye run is one of the most famous fish migrations in North America.

name: St. Clair River

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Spring spawn run from Lake St. Clair into Lake Huron.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Heavy current = trophy smallmouth on bottom structure.

species.name: Lake sturgeon
species.notes: Catch + release.

patterns.title: Spring + fall walleye vertical jigging
patterns.technique: Vertical jigging 1/2-1 oz jigheads in heavy current
patterns.where: Below Algonac; the St. Clair Flats delta area

notes: Connects Lake Huron + Lake St. Clair. Strong-current river with trophy walleye + sturgeon.

name: Saginaw Bay (Lake Huron)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: One of the most productive walleye fisheries in the Great Lakes. Self-sustaining population.

species.name: Yellow perch
species.notes: World-class jumbo perch when populations cycle high.

patterns.title: Trolling walleye on the open bay
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + bottom bouncers; trolling Reef Runners + Husky Jerks on planer boards
patterns.where: Inner Bay flats 12-22 ft; the Outer Bay 25-40 ft in summer
patterns.details: Saginaw Bay walleye fishery is genuinely world-class. Multiple year-class booms in recent years. Charter scene is robust out of Bay City + Caseville + Linwood.

patterns.title: Jumbo perch summer + fall
patterns.technique: Crappie minnows + emerald shiners on perch rigs; small jigs + waxworms
patterns.where: Mid-bay over hard bottoms; the gravel shoals

managementProgram: MI DNR Saginaw Bay walleye management
notes: Inner Bay is shallow + warm (10-30 ft); Outer Bay is deeper + colder. Walleye + perch fishery is the heart of MI Lake Huron angling.

name: Saginaw River

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Spring walleye run from Saginaw Bay.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Spring run.

patterns.title: Spring walleye run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 3/8-1/2 oz; small swimbaits
patterns.where: Bay City + below the Center Avenue Dam in Saginaw
patterns.details: Saginaw walleye stage to spawn in late March-April. Less famous than Detroit but quality.

notes: Formed by confluence of the Tittabawassee + Shiawassee + Cass + Flint rivers. Drains the largest watershed in MI.

name: Tittabawassee River

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Spring run staging here is iconic — fish move up from Saginaw Bay.

patterns.title: Spring walleye below Sanford Dam
patterns.technique: Jigging plastics + minnows; small swimbaits
patterns.where: Below the (rebuilt) Sanford Dam; Midland area
patterns.details: The Sanford + Tittabawassee dams failed catastrophically in 2020. Rebuilding ongoing. Walleye habitat is shifting as the system recovers.

notes: The "Tittabawassee" run is part of the Saginaw walleye system. 2020 dam failure changed the river's character — recovery is ongoing.

name: Grand River
signatureSpecies: King salmon, steelhead, walleye, smallmouth bass

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up from Lake Michigan to the 6th Street Dam in Grand Rapids.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Spring + fall runs.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Below dams (Lyons, Lansing).

patterns.title: Fall salmon at 6th Street Dam (Grand Rapids)
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners; bobbers + skein
patterns.where: Below 6th Street Dam in downtown Grand Rapids
patterns.details: Salmon stage below the 6th Street Dam in the heart of downtown Grand Rapids. Urban salmon fishing — most-fished hole in the SLP.

patterns.title: Spring walleye below dams
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/4 oz
patterns.where: Below Lyons + Portland + Lansing dams

notes: MI's longest river. Flows from Jackson through Lansing + Grand Rapids to Lake Michigan at Grand Haven. Urban salmon fishery in downtown GR.

name: Kalamazoo River
signatureSpecies: King salmon, steelhead, smallmouth bass, brown trout

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up to Allegan Dam.

patterns.title: Salmon below Allegan Dam
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners
patterns.where: Allegan Dam tailwater

notes: Tailwater fishing below Allegan Dam. Recovery from Enbridge oil spill (2010) is ongoing.

name: St. Joseph River (Berrien County)
signatureSpecies: King salmon, steelhead, brown trout, smallmouth bass

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up to Berrien Springs Dam.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Skamania + winter strain.

patterns.title: Salmon staging at Berrien Springs Dam
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners; spey casting
patterns.where: Berrien Springs Dam tailwater

notes: NOT the same St. Joseph River as the South Bend one (different watershed). This one drains to Lake Michigan at St. Joseph, MI.

name: Au Sable River — Lower (Oscoda)

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up from Lake Huron.

patterns.title: Fall Au Sable salmon (Foote Dam)
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners
patterns.where: Below Foote Dam (Iosco County) downstream to Oscoda
patterns.details: The Lake Huron side of the Au Sable. Foote Dam is the limit of upstream salmon passage. Less crowded than Lake Michigan tribs.

notes: The Au Sable's lower 12 miles below Foote Dam is salmon + walleye water. Distinct from the Holy Water (upper Au Sable trout system).
